macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Fed’s Preferred Inflation Measure Jumps to 3.4% as Core PCE Rises in May
items.summary: The Federal Reserve’s preferred inflation gauge, core PCE, increased to 3.4% in May from 3.3% in April, driven by higher energy prices, while the overall PCE price index rose 4.1% annually.

items.title: Consumer Spending Hits All-Time High of $21.90 Trillion as PCE Grows 0.7% in May
items.summary: Personal consumption expenditures rose 0.7% month-over-month in May, reaching an annual rate of $21.90 trillion, marking the highest level ever recorded according to BEA data.

items.title: Gas Prices Fall Below $4 to $3.91 as Energy Costs Ease Despite Inflation Spike
items.summary: The national average for a gallon of regular gasoline declined to $3.91, down from $3.99 last week, even as higher energy prices contributed to a jump in the Fed’s preferred inflation measure.

items.title: Unemployment Claims Drop to 215,000 as Private Job Growth Remains Stable at 30,750
items.summary: Initial unemployment claims fell to 215,000 in the week ending June 20, while ADP reported private employers added 30,750 jobs in the four weeks ending June 6, signaling labor market stability.

items.title: Atlanta Fed GDPNow Model Projects 2.5% Real GDP Growth for Q2 2026
items.summary: Near-term GDP growth estimates are tracking positively, with the Atlanta Fed’s GDPNow model forecasting real GDP growth at a 2.5% rate in the second quarter of 2026.

items.title: Global Equity Markets Decline 9% Amid Middle East Conflict and Energy Supply Crisis
items.summary: Global equity markets fell 9% from late February to end-March 2026, with the S&P 500 down 8%, as the Middle East conflict and closure of the Strait of Hormuz triggered energy supply shocks and inflation surges.

items.title: AI Boom Consumes $1 Trillion Annually, Crowding Out Other Sectors and Driving Inflation
items.summary: Artificial intelligence expansion is expected to exceed $1 trillion annually by next year, capturing nearly two-thirds of global venture capital in 2025 and contributing to shortages in housing, energy, and supply chains that fuel inflation.

items.title: Mortgage Rates Rise to 6.49% as Newly Built Home Sales Drop 7.3% in May
items.summary: The average 30-year fixed mortgage rate increased to 6.49%, while sales of newly built homes declined 7.3% to an annualized rate of 580,000 units, reflecting tightening housing market conditions.

items.title: Business Investment Hits Record $84.0 Billion as Core Capex Rises 1.6% in May
items.summary: Orders for nondefense capital goods excluding aircraft rose 1.6% to a record $84.0 billion in May, signaling strong business investment despite broader economic pressures.

items.title: Central Banks Expected to Raise Rates Amid Inflation Surge Following Middle East Conflict
items.summary: Central banks in the U.S., euro area, U.K., and Canada are now expected to raise interest rates rather than ease, as inflation compensation surged globally due to energy supply shocks from the Middle East conflict.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Hovers Near $60,000 as Analysts Signal Potential Market Bottom
items.summary: Bitcoin is trading just below $60,000 (approximately $59,660) with technical indicators like MACD flipping slightly, suggesting the crypto market bottom may be near despite a 50% drawdown from its $126,000 all-time high eight months ago.

items.title: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse Criticizes Michael Saylor's Strategy While Staying Bullish on Bitcoin
items.summary: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse stated in a recent interview that while he remains bullish on Bitcoin, Michael Saylor's strategy has negatively impacted the broader crypto market, citing Saylor's recent lies and forced selling to meet yield obligations.

items.title: Securitize Announces SPAC Merger to Go Public Ahead of Clarity Act
items.summary: Tokenization firm Securitize is set to go public this week via a SPAC merger rather than an IPO, with timing potentially linked to the upcoming Clarity Act expected to reach the Senate floor in July.

items.title: Ripple Launches Capitol Hill Ad Campaign and Branded Trucks to Urge Clarity Act Passage
items.summary: Ripple is actively lobbying for the Clarity Act with a new Capitol Hill ad campaign featuring branded trucks and slogans like 'On the Road to Clarity,' while reportedly investing significant resources in Washington DC.

items.title: Better Mortgage Pledges $250M in Crypto Loans Without Capital Gains Taxes
items.summary: Better Mortgage (formerly Better) is launching a nationwide rollout this summer for a crypto loan program allowing borrowers to pledge BTC or USDC without triggering capital gains taxes or facing margin calls, protecting $250 million in initial loan volume.

items.title: Chainlink Breaks Below Previous Lows While Solana Shows Bullish Divergence
items.summary: In recent altcoin movements, Chainlink is breaking below previous candle lows with significant resistance between $8 and $8.50, while Solana continues to play out a bullish divergence pattern.

items.title: Ethereum Tests Support Between $1,500 and $1,600 Amid Short-Term Struggle
items.summary: Ethereum is testing a strong support area between $1,500 and $1,600, struggling at short-term resistance levels similar to Bitcoin's current position near $58,500 support.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Unveils GPT-5.6 Model Family with Sol, Terra, and Luna Variants
items.summary: OpenAI has officially launched the next-generation GPT-5.6 model family, featuring specialized variants named Sol, Terra, and Luna, which show massive benchmark improvements over GPT-5.5 and compete with Claude Fable 5.

items.title: U.S. Government Mandates Staggered Rollout for GPT-5.6 Access
items.summary: The United States government has requested a staggered, customer-by-customer rollout of GPT-5.6 access, with OpenAI agreeing to the staggered approval process amid national security concerns and the AI race with China.

items.title: Anthropic Overtakes OpenAI in Enterprise AI Adoption
items.summary: New industry data reveals that Anthropic has surpassed OpenAI in enterprise AI adoption, while rumors suggest Anthropic is close to restoring access to its temporarily embargoed Fable 5 model.

items.title: Zhipu AI Releases New Cybersecurity Model Matching Claude Mythos
items.summary: Zhipu AI claims its newest model can match the performance of Claude Mythos specifically in cybersecurity tasks, marking a significant breakthrough in specialized AI security applications.

items.title: Grok 4.5 Under Private Testing at SpaceX and Tesla
items.summary: Rumors indicate that Grok 4.5 is already being privately tested inside SpaceX and Tesla, suggesting accelerated integration of advanced AI models into critical aerospace and automotive infrastructure.

items.title: Perplexity CEO Predicts AI Speciation Over Monolithic Models
items.summary: Perplexity's CEO argues that AI models are specializing for different niches rather than merging into one brain, predicting a future of 'speciation' where different AI 'brains' evolve for specific industries.

items.title: Cognition Raises $26 Billion Valuation for AI Software Engineer Devin
items.summary: Cognition, the company behind the world's first AI software engineer Devin, raised funding in May 2026 at a $26 billion valuation, with CEO Scott Wu predicting AI will solve most human-hour tasks within five years.

items.title: Open-Source AI Threatens Token Business Models as Frontier Labs Push Bans
items.summary: Industry analysis suggests that open-source AI models approaching frontier intelligence levels threaten the token-based business model, leading some major AI labs to potentially advocate for banning open-source AI.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: GTA 6 Pre-Orders Live, Confirming November 19 Release Date
items.summary: Rockstar Games has enabled pre-orders for GTA 6, signaling strong confidence in hitting the November 19, 2026 launch date. The move implies the game is nearing completion and ready for consumer purchase ahead of release.

items.title: Bungie Announces Major Layoffs Targeting Destiny Team
items.summary: Bungie has confirmed significant layoffs with cuts impacting most of the Destiny development team, marking a dire shift for the franchise's future. The layoffs include multiple senior staff members being let go.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Remake Reviews Set for July 8
items.summary: IGN released a first look at the remade Havana city in Assassin's Creed Black Flag, showcasing improved NPCs and city design. Reviews for the remake will go live one day before launch on July 8, 2026.

items.title: 2027 Game Slate Fills Up with Fable, Tomb Raider, and Persona 4
items.summary: Early 2027 is becoming crowded with major titles including Fable, Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis, and Persona 4 Revival all planned for February. Jason Schreier also expects Law Fay to launch in February or March.

items.title: Xbox Raises Prices Again Amid Industry Shifts
items.summary: Microsoft has announced another price increase for Xbox services, continuing a trend of rising costs in the gaming industry. The move comes alongside broader discussions about Steam Machine pricing and PS6 implications.

items.title: Teen Hacker Arion Kurtaj Still Serving Indefinite Sentence for GTA 6 Leak
items.summary: Arion Kurtaj, the teenager who hacked GTA 6 in 2022 using basic devices like a mobile phone and hotel TV, remains in a secure hospital prison under an indefinite sentence. The leak was one of the biggest in gaming history.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Valve Unlocks Steam Machine: Users Can Build Custom SteamOS PCs
items.summary: Valve confirmed that the latest SteamOS update allows users to build their own Steam Machines, a long-term project now compatible with AMD hardware and featuring FSR 4.1 support for Radeon RX 7000 series graphics.

items.title: Bolt Graphics Enters Market as New Gaming GPU Challenger to NVIDIA
items.summary: A new gaming GPU brand, Bolt Graphics, has launched as a direct competitor to NVIDIA, signaling increased competition in the high-performance graphics card sector.

items.title: AMD Releases FSR 4.1 Upscaling for Radeon RX 7000 Series
items.summary: AMD Gaming officially released FSR 4.1 upscaling technology for its Radeon RX 7000 series graphics cards, enhancing visual fidelity for gamers.

items.title: Google Introduces 24-Hour Wait for Android App Installs from Unverified Developers
items.summary: Starting in September, Google will enforce a 24-hour waiting period for installing apps from unverified developers on Android devices, requiring users to enable developer mode for sideloading.
